---
title: "Project 1"
author: "Jacob Kebe"
output: pdf_document
documentclass: article
geometry: margin=1in
fontsize: 12pt
linestretch: 2
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{setspace}
  - \doublespacing
  - \usepackage{multirow}
  - \usepackage{amsmath}
  - \usepackage{amssymb}
  - \usepackage{dcolumn}
  - \usepackage{fvextra}
  - \usepackage{bm}
---



```{r ggplots}
library(gridExtra)
grid.arrange(p_lvl0, p_leu3n0, p_aggment0, p_aggphys0, p_lvl2, p_leu3n2, p_aggment2, p_aggphys2, nrow=2, ncol=4)

```
# Introduction

Approximately 0.5 page.
• Briefly describe the project including:
o data received from the investigator
o the scientific hypothesis of interest
• Rephrase the scientific hypothesis of interest into testable statistical hypotheses.
• Note this is NOT a scientific introduction to a paper and should give information
pertinent to the data analysis, not more general biology background.


# Methods
• Approximately 2 pages.
• Should be written in past tense and should NOT include any results.
• A brief description of data management and any methods used to clean the data. This
may include, but is not limited to:
o Methods used to identify outliers or potentially erroneous values.
o Describing any new variables you calculated from the dataset, for example,
classifying subjects based on a continuous variable or calculating BMI from
height and weight.
o Describing any filtering of the dataset, for example limiting the dataset to the time
range and/or population of interest to address the research question
• An in-depth description of methods used to analyze the data. This may include, but is not
limited to:
o A brief description of preliminary data analysis methods and descriptive statistic
methods.
o A description of the statistical model used to analyze the data
▪ Type of model
▪ Outcome variable
▪ Primary explanatory variables
▪ Potential covariates, including confounders, mediators, effect modifiers,
and precision variables
Name
Date
- 3 -
o If you are unsure of which variables will be included in your model a priori,
describe your model selection strategy
o Describe how you will test the hypotheses stated in the introduction.
▪ For example, in a regression modelling setting, which coefficients or
combination of coefficients will you test?
▪ What type of statistical test will you use (t-test, F-test, Chi-square test,
likelihood ratio test…)?
▪ What level test will be used to determine statistical significance?
▪ Did you adjust for multiple comparisons?
• Justify and explain your data analysis approach
o How does this approach address the research question?
o Why is this approach appropriate for the type of data you are analyzing?
• Do NOT include equations, unless completely necessary. Any equations must be written
with correct statistical notation and fully explained in terms a non-statistician can
understand.



# Results
• Approximately 1-1.5 pages
• Present results for analyses described in the methods. In addition to describing the results
of statistical testing this includes:
o Descriptive statistics
o If model selection strategies were use, the variables included in the final model
• Use Tables and Figures as appropriate
Name
Date
- 4 -
• Include full interpretation of statistical results for the main findings in the text, including:
o point estimates
o confidence bounds
o p-values
o interpretation of the results of tests


# Conclusions / Discussion
• 0.5-1 page
• Interpret your results in context of scientific question(s).
• Discuss any limitations of your analysis that may affect interpretation or that require
additional consideration by the investigator
